title: crepis × holubyana
intro: Crepis × holubyana, also known as Holuby's hawk's beard, is a hybrid plant species within the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is commonly found in the mountainous regions of Central Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, thanks to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including anti-cancer, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory effects, which may contribute to its use in traditional medicine. Crepis × holubyana can be prepared for medicinal use in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of various gastrointestinal disorders.
